trend_name: Bella Hadid Just Made the ‘Pageboy Bob’ Spring’s Coolest Haircut
description:

No, that’s not anOlsen tuck. Bella Hadid just debuted a major chop at Paris Fashion Week—and convinced us that thepageboy bobis going to be spring’s trendiest haircut.
Clearly, the 29-year-old model has had an itch for a hair transformation recently. Last week shedebutednew wispy bangs with her sandy,low-maintenance blondecolor. Since then herlong layershave been trimmed up to her chin. And while she’s definitely giving Parisian-chic energy, this look has a little more edge than the romanticFrench bob.
A cut with ’60s roots, “the traditional pageboy bob features a rounded, ear-to-shoulder length with inward-curled blunt ends and a heavy fringe,”Elliot Bute, a stylist at London salon Hershesons previously toldGlamour.While OG pageboy bobs (think Princess Diana) had a distinctly rounded shape, modern renditions like Hadid’s “add varied lengths and textured finishes, transforming the once-rigid style into something more versatile,” Bute says.
Unlike ablunt bobwhich favors sharp lines, the softer pageboy bob invites tousled texture. That’s what makes it an effortlessshort haircutfor spring and summer: It’s short enough to keep things cool but not so precise that you’ll need to tote around yourflatiron. While this look can be styled sleek, it looks just as intentional with a hit oftexture sprayand some natural bends.
Feeling inspired by Bella Hadid’s new look? The good news is there are plenty of ways to customize the pageboy bob to your unique face shape and hair type. Bute highly recommends experimenting with fringe for this cut: Opt for wispywisteria bangsfor something low maintenance or turn up the face-framing flattery with a thickercurtain fringe.
You can also get the essence of the look without committing to a super-short cut. “Play with different lengths—from chin-length toshoulder-length,” Bute recommends. Midlength haircuts more your speed? “Aim for a length that sits around the shoulders and layers that highlight facial features and add movement,” he says.
Grace McCarty is the associate beauty editor atGlamour.

Abobcan be a lazy girl's dream style until it you need your hair out of your face. And while some can work magic with a claw clip, others struggle to avoid lumps and bumps without invoking gels and wax. Sure,slick-back styleshave dominated hair trends in the past few years, but they take time to perfect, candamage your hair, and are hardly appropriate for the gym.
Hailey Bieber'ssolution to claw clip bumps? Embrace them. On March 7, Bieber proved the claw-clip is gym-friendly during a weight-lifting workout with celebrity trainer Kirsty Godso. Instead of pulling her shoulder-length bob back into a ponytail, she gave herself a middle part before securing the top half of her hair away from her face with a simple black claw-clip. That's it. That's the whole style.
While Bieber can hardly be expected to work out the lumps and bumps to get her “ass kicked” by Godso, she let her lay just as naturally when she got dolled up in a chic red polka dot going out top.
WATCH THIS
Pat McGrath Reacts to Recreations of Her Iconic Makeup Looks
In her latest Instagram post, Bieber posed for a series of selfies to show off her latest Rhode launches, but her blush combo wasn't the only beauty technique we'll be copying this spring. She seemingly sported the same half-up style that she wore to the gym, though she released a face-framing front piece for an extra feminine touch.
Over onTikTok, she shared her blush and lip routine featuring Rhode's new spring shades. Using a dual-sided brush, Bieber started by applying her pocket blush in the raspberry pink shade teacup across her cheekbone and the bridge of her nose before adding a touch of candy apple red to the highpoint of the apple of her cheek before blending out. She then used a beige-toned lip shape to outline her lip, which she blurred out with her fingertip before finishing off the look with her peptide lip tint in the new “shimmering warm mauve” shade pretzel.
While it's possible Hailey Bieber replaced her claw clip from the gym with a couple of bobby pins or barrettes, the process would be just as quick with the same end result: a soft, feminine look that compliments herberry girl makeuproutine. And it probably took her less than five seconds.

trend_name: HowLove StoryRecreated Carolyn Bessette Kennedy’s Iconic ’90s Blonde Hair
description:

Additional options regarding these technologies may be available on your device, browser, or through industry options like AdChoices. Please see our Privacy Policy for more information.

Of the many style influencesCarolyn Bessette Kennedyleft us with, one of the most recognizable is her famous sunny blonde hair. Whether it was tousled by hand or pulled back in atortoiseshell headband, Bessette’s length and color followed the same effortless code ofher wardrobe, leaving us with enduringhair inspirationeven after her untimely passing.
Following the premiere ofLove Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette,there’s been renewed interest in CBK’s ’90s blonde; with its dark roots, golden streaks, and effortless texture, the look is difficult to recreate in today’s era of endless tools and salon services. Case in point: Thoseearly images from theLove Storyset, which sparked outrage from fans andcriticism from Bessette’s coloristfor its styling inaccuracy.
Love Storyhair designerBarry Lee Moewent to extreme lengths to get the texture and color just right for actress Sarah Pidgeon, who portrays Bessette in the limited series. The process began with a big obstacle: Upon being cast, Pidgeon had a dark brunette bob, tasking Moe with the ultimate hair transformation.
WATCH THIS
Blake Lively, Anna Kendrick & 'Another Simple Favor' Cast Take a Friendship Test
“When I came onto the project, I came on a little bit late and we knew that we wanted to use her own hair,” says Moe. “So the starting point was, How do we get this beautiful young actress from a dark brown bob to Carolyn Bessette’s wavy, dimensional ’90s blonde?”
Sarah Pidgeon as Carolyn Bessette
Moe enlisted the best in the business, first askingTracey Cunningham, a go-to colorist for celebs likeJennifer Garner,Emma Stone, andDakota Johnson, for advice. Cunningham immediately connected him with coloristKari Hilland hairstylistAlex Pardoe, who collaborated with Moe on the final look.
“Kari and Alex were absolutely crucial in making this happen because we couldn’t do it with clip-in extensions,” Moe says. "We couldn’t do it with a wig. It needed to be her own hair, and it needed to look like her own hair. We were really concerned about the health of Sarah’s hair, knowing that it was going to be such a transformation.”
The end result? Two blonde looks that faithfully mirrored Bessette’s evolution. “So in the earlier years of her relationship with John, the color was carefree, with more dimension and rogue pieces,” says Hill. “And then towards the later ’90s, it became less dimensional. And that was also a very buttery golden blonde. Both so beautiful, both had its place. I love the younger, carefree dimensional blonde and the sophisticated, calm, cool, collected blonde that just went with her entire wardrobe from head to toe. And it was just so depicting of what her life was.”
Ahead, Moe and Hill share the reference photos they used of Bessette, the intense process of Pidgeon’s hair transformation, and the surprisingly simple technique used to style her effortless waves.
Glamour: Sarah Pidgeon started the show with basically the opposite of Carolyn’s long wavy blonde hair. How did you pull that off transformation so seamlessly?
Barry Lee Moe:We hadK-Tip bonded extensionspermanently bonded to Sarah’s hair. Alex Pardoe has his own hairline calledThe Anti Co, and all of the hair is virgin, sourced from Ukraine. The hair had a natural, beautiful wave. We used about four or five different colors, and the placement was key. But it all started with getting Sarah’s own hair to a color that we could use to add the extensions into. So we had to start with color first, and then move into the extensions. We tried to always count whenever we would redo them because it was a fun game. Sarah loved to count them and be like, “How many do we have?”
Pidgeon on June 14, 2025
Pidgeon on July 14, 2025
Do you know how many were used, ballpark estimate?
Barry:It ranged between 300 and 400 individual bonded extensions. When you’re working with a blunt bob that’s really short, the blend on that is nearly impossible. In order to successfully achieve the right density and blend, we had to use a lot of small individual extensions so that it moves like her real hair. We could pull it up into ponytails, she could flip it, and we would never see evidence of any extensions.
Kari, can you speak to this transformation of taking Sarah from such a dark color to this sunny, dimensional blonde?
Kari Hill:We needed her to do different phases throughout the show that needed to be used in many different years and versions. So we had to start very low and slow. I say that because it takes a lot of time and application of color. The process has to be very tedious to keep the hair healthy and to keep it quote-unquote on her head so that she then has hair that can be styled in the way that Carolyn had. I didn’t want to damage the hair and have breakage and then hand her over to Barry to style it on set and not have those iconic long pieces in the front. Those are her hair. Those aren’t extensions. So we needed to keep it healthy.
How long was the process getting her to that blonde?
Barry:In total, it was probably close to 20 hours of color. We did it over the course of two days, and Alex had to put the extensions in in that timeframe as well, but the extensions going in is much quicker. Because once the color’s there, Alex can knock in the placement and it’s about three to four hours to put in the extensions. After that, we did more color work because we really wanted to fine-tune and make sure it all worked together.
After the extensions, you did more color work? Wow.
Barry:After the extensions, yeah. We did two full days, about 12 to 14 hours each day, in the salon. It was intense, but it was worth it because her hair came out just as healthy as it was when we started. I was noticing last night, just looking at her on the red carpet, her hair is still very healthy today. That’s a true testament to how careful and considerate Kari was when she did the work.
Kari, can you speak more about keeping Sarah’s hair healthy? Were there any products that helped?
Kari:Keeping in mind the health of her hair and what we were just talking about those days and those hours, that’s the first transformation. So I had a second going lighter. And through each one, I did useSchwarzkopf BlondMe Lightener, which has bonders and vitamins in that and also the glosses. I have used Schwarzkopf my whole career. We call it foiled cashmere, but it’s the application of my foils that I put in her hair to re-create both looks. And then it’s about your formula. It’s about choosing the right color to do these root glosses, the shadows. She has a very natural dark hair color, so to keep it more natural and from looking too artificial, I would do root glosses on her to tide her over in between each touch-up.
Barry:So much of the color process, especially with recreating someone's likeness, is so organic. It’s like making a painting together as a group effort because we started with this canvas. We had to do corrective priming work, and then all of the foils and the lightning and lifting and then the extensions went in. Even though we didn’t want to do more color, after the extensions went in, we were like, “We have to do these bold chunky pieces. That’s missing.” So then Kari went in and hand-painted these incredible big chunks that you see really pop on camera. Those came in the final round of color, in the last few hours of our crazy two-day experience. We just really leaned on each other to step back and look and say, “It’s good, but it could be better.” We pushed ourselves to the limits to make sure that we really went there.
Kari, what would you call this color? If someone wanted to go into the salon and ask for a similar look, what should they say?
Kari:There is a technique, foil cashmere, and there’s two glossing techniques. I have one that I did on her in the earlier scenes, and I used Schwarzkopf Igora Vibrance glosses and toners for both, but I call that first one gradient toning because her hair goes from darker to lighter up to the hairline, like it did in the ’90s. Specifically in the early ’90s, the hair was just much more dimensional, so contrasting and rogue pieces. It was just so carefree to a very much more tailored and bespoke glossing technique.
Both are techniques that are inSchwarzkopf’s Educational Academy. If a colorist wants to learn what this is, if a client goes in and says, “I want to get this blonde,” they can go and learn it. We have put the education out there so they can go get certified online free at Schwarzkopf Professional Academy.
Barry, were there any photos of Carolyn that immediately came to mind for reference?
Barry:There were so many images. We had our research team on production create this incredible visual timeline from the beginning of the piece to the end of the piece, which was hundreds and hundreds of images. You can get lost in which images you respond to. We really had to go to some of those early, early images. There’s one of her sitting at Indochine with the iconic wallpaper with her friends at a booth, and you just see the dimension in her color, and it’s all tossed to the side. You really get a lot of energy from that photo.
Bessette at Indochine in 1994.
There’s also thisearly Polaroidof her where it’s just her standing dead-on, looking straight at the camera, and her hair is wild and tousled, and her eyes are just haunting and beautifully light. The other thing I responded to was the random organic nature of her texture. It never was uniform. It always looked like she let it air-dry, but she blew out the top just to make sure that this was smooth, but then the rest was wild. It was trying to find that balance, so that it didn’t feel overly styled, but it also could be consistent for the purposes of filmmaking.
How did you recreate Carolyn’s effortless waves?
Barry:First and foremost, I made sure that the hair of the extensions was textured, because Sarah’s natural texture is not as prominent as what you see on camera. Alex and I chose hair fromAnti Co, Alex’s extension company, that had this beautiful natural wave to it so we had a great foundation for setting these waves each day. Then we had to make sure that it was always blended with Sarah’s natural hair. If you iron it, you’re always going to have a disconnect between her bob and the extensions, especially because her bob was so short.
Braiding it off every night was the most successful way to blend the extensions into the natural hair and create that beautiful soft wave. We would split her hair into four quadrants, two in the front, two in the back, and just do loose braids withUnite Hair 7Seconds Detangler Leave-Into keep moisture in there but also help set the wave. When we took that out, we had this really beautiful natural texture. Having her sleep in it also created some natural frizz. You get some of that natural volume. Then I would go through and enhance it just a bit with a waving iron to pop sections that didn’t take the braid as strongly, just even it out.
Amazon
And then I would go through as well with some tapered vertical spirals that I would drag some curls throughout just to break up the wave with some random curls here and there so it didn’t feel like we had approached the whole thing uniformly. It was a little bit random, and it was just kind of trying to manipulate that each day. I would always finish it off with having her flip her hair upside down. We'd spray texturizing spray through it, shake it out, and then have her flip it up so that we had that really nice foundational volume and texture there. I always tell girls that have straight hair: Braid your hair wet, and you’re going to have this amazing texture the next day.
Can you speak to how the styling choices conveyed the passage of time and how it lends itself to the storyline?
Barry:The big thing for styling was to show a clear transition from her younger days before JFK Jr., and then to the time that they were in a relationship together. When you look at the visual timeline, you can see very clearly that there was a shift when they started dating. When they got more serious, her look got more serious. And when the paparazzi got more intense, she got more guarded. Everything was more uniform, more sleek, and it felt more controlled than when she was younger. Every photo that exists of her when she’s younger, it’s wild. You can tell she’s just flipped her hair. You can tell she’s let it air-dry, that she’s maybe blown out sections of it, but not all of it. There was a chaos to it—this wild, carefree energy. And then later in the series, everything’s all about sleek, smooth styling. More uniform. And also, a reflection of where she was at financially.
She was in a different situation then. When she started her relationship with JFK Jr., things shifted. She was in a different world. She was around different people. It was kind of the Kennedy effect. So we went from the more natural textured styling to classic ’90s blowouts, smooth, sleek styling to really show a shift and erase that former version of herself.
What do you hope audiences take away from the styling of this show?
It’s such a tricky thing to talk about because this whole experience has been amazing for us. We’ve created this beautiful imagery in this series with her hair, but we’re also talking about someone who passed away in a tragic way and is suddenly becoming this mainstream figure where people are trying to emulate her looks and recreate this and this.
It’s exciting to be part of that, but it also reminds you of what this piece is about, which is this tragic love story. I hope that people are inspired by the work, but that they’re also more informed about the tragedy of the story to begin with. What we can take away from it is that Carolyn was someone who moved through the world without any intention to impact people with the way that she looked, but she did so effortlessly. In a time when the world is surrounded with this uniformity, we revisit the value of just showing up as yourself, as who you are, and how powerful that can be in a world full of people trying to conform. The magic of Carolyn was that she was luxury, but she was lived-in luxury and she wasn’t trying. She was just existing.
We can try to re-create that all we want, but it’s really digging within yourself and asking, How do I want to show up in this world? Carolyn can teach us to embrace the things you love, put on the jacket that feels good to you, and don’t give a shit about what anyone else thinks because showing up as your authentic self is the best way. And you will impact people as a result.
Ariana Yaptangco is the senior beauty editor atGlamour.Follow her@arianayap.

trend_name: The 15 Best Short Wedding Hairstyles to Wear Down the Aisle
description:

While some brides invest in hair extensions for their big day, short wedding hairstyles can be just as romantic and elegant. Whether you want to stay true to yoursignature bobor are considering a big chop before your wedding, rest assured: You’ll still have plenty of swoon-worthy styles to choose from.
“I honestly love short bridal hair. It feels so chic and intentional,” saysHaven Whiteman, pro stylist at Luxy Hair. “When a bride chooses to keep her hair short on her wedding day, it reads as confident and modern but still timeless. It also really shows off the neckline, the dress details, and the face in a way that longer hair sometimes hides.”
One prime example? Selena Gomez’sOld Hollywood wedding bob, which earned a permanent spot on our mood boards after herSeptember 2025 nuptials. To get the look, stylistRenato Camporatold us he styled Gomez’sshoulder-length hair“in a marcel wave pattern, pinning each curl to cool and hold its shape.” The final look was timeless and lasted well past the star-studded reception.
“Short hair can bevoluminous, tousled, sleek, polished, or romantic. It just depends on the direction you take it,” Whiteman says. As to which short bridal styles are trending in 2026? According to the expert: “TheFrench girl, effortless vibe is still very in. Bobs, especially, are having a moment. Brides want movement and texture—hair that feels soft and touchable.”
Feeling inspired? Keep scrolling for all our favorite short wedding hairstyles—and get ready to book your trial appointment.
For a balanced short wedding hairstyle that works well with a veil, a half-up look is an ideal choice. “For brides who want something soft but still styled, a short,wavy half-uplook with delicate pins is stunning,” Whiteman says. “It keeps the face open but still shows movement."
You can never go wrong with a big, bouncy blowout for a special occasion. “There’s also something powerful about keeping your natural length and just enhancing it. If someone wants a little more fullness without adding length, something subtle likeThinning Fill-Insfrom Luxy can make a big difference,” Whiteman says. “It just gives the style more body, so it photographs beautifully without looking overdone.”
As Emma Stone routinely proves, short hair can still be styled into a beautifulupdo effect. The trick is to pin back your pieces strategically while maintaining soft texture (and accommodating your veil, if you’re wearing one). With this look, you can also have some fun with your front pieces. Stone had hers styled as romanticwisteria bangs, which frame the face perfectly.
Looking for natural hair inspo for your big day? This side-swept look on Issa Rae is perfect fortype 4 curls. It maintains ethereal volume at the crown while gathering the hair into an elegant low-bun shape at the nape of the neck. Talk about timeless.
Selena Gomez’s bridal hair was so good, she’s put it on repeat for countless red-carpet occasions. For all the details—from hair prep to hold products—check out stylist Renato Campora’sfull breakdown of the lookforGlamour.
Planning a Gatsby-esque bash for your big day? Channel somevintage energywith finger waves—a timeless, sculpted style that brings both edge and glamour to your bridal look. This style works especially well forpixie cutsand close-cropped curls.
The trendy bixie cut—halfway between a traditional pixie and a bob—lends itself particularly well to ethereal wedding styles. Copy Florence Pugh’s red-carpet look with a deep side part and soft, swooping waves.
Want to keep your hair out of your face but don’t have enough length for an intricate updo? Bring in some pearl or jewel pins to add more intrigue.
If you’re opting for a sleek wedding hairstyle but still looking for a little softness, consider incorporating asculpted fringe. Taylor Russell’s rendition is clean and minimalistic, while bringing in some face-framing flattery.
Blunt bobsare all the rage in 2026—and that includes bridal looks. A straight bob with clean lines is the perfect offset to your softer wedding details. Plus, as this Emma Chamberlain look proves, it looks effortlessly cool with a bridal headband.
Vintage influenceis the easiest way to ensure that your wedding photos age well, and Raye’s soft curls are the perfect reference. Focus on creating volume and texture at your ends for a full-bodied silver screen shape.
A shorter length doesn’t have to mean sacrificing fullness in your bridal style.Cloud curlsare the most angelic way to embrace your natural texture. According to hairstylistTom Smith, who coined the name of the trend, cloud curls intentionally form “a halo of gentle volume.” “They’re less about defined ringlets and more about diffused texture and natural expansion. The look is ethereal and floaty,” he says.
The perfect happy medium between cool and romantic, the sleek bridal bob should be “smooth, shiny, and clean with barely there bends,” Whiteman says. “It’s minimal but so timeless. This pairs beautifully with structured gowns or statement earrings because it keeps everything looking refine.” For added flare, try a vintage forehead curl like Lily Collins's.
For the low-key bride, a simple lob allows for “airy volume with natural movement,” Whiteman says. “Slightly tousled but still polished, it feels romantic without trying too hard.” She says this look is “perfect with off-the-shoulder gowns or anything delicate.” If you’re looking for something simple, flattering, and versatile, the romantic lob is for you.
Why leave all your florals to your bouquet? Add some sprigs of baby’s breath to your half-up style, or opt for a full boho flower crown. Shorter lengths balance out a statement

I’ll let you in on a secret: I rarely wear foundation; instead I rely on the best tinted moisturizers for lightweight, radiant coverage.What exactly is a tinted moisturizer, you ask? It’s a multitasker that serves as both skin care and makeup, so you get the best of both worlds. And no matter your coverage preference, these pigmented hydrators—which can includeBB creams,CC creams, and skin tints—have skin-boosting ingredients that can target specific concerns.
To help you find your perfect match, we curated the list below featuringGlamourBeauty Award winnersand tested options from dermatologists and makeup artists as well asGlamour-editor favorites. Our top pick overall is theLaura Mercier Tinted Moisturizerbecause it really does last all day, is suitable for all skin types, comes in a variety of shades, and pairs well with other products. We also found amazing drugstore buys, plenty of options packed with SPF, and a product our testers with mature skin loved.

The best part about tinted moisturizers is how well they keep your skin hydrated and fresh all day long. Specs:
Amazon
Nordstrom
FiveGlamoureditorstried Kosas BB Burst Tinted Gel Creammoisturizer and completely fell in love. The formula is a gel-cream that hydrates skin with ingredients such as collagen-boosting copper peptides, smoothing saccharide isomerate, moisture-retaining sodium PCA, and calming zinc oxide. It comes in an impressive 24-shade range and promises buildable coverage with a smooth and natural finish.
Testing notes:
“This BB cream has been a staple in my beauty routine during the warmer months,” saysGlamour’s Kilkeary. “It has a creamy texture and dries down into a soft matte finish that’s a bit radiant but not overly shiny. It takes a few layers to get full coverage on acne-prone areas, so I would definitely recommend it if you’re looking for a lighter finish, but it adds a nice plump and glow to the skin overall.”
Specs:
Amazon
Sephora
Boost your skin’s natural look and texture with this lightweight gel skin tint that’s suitable for all skin types, from dry to oily and everything in between. The formulation blends hyaluronic acid, niacinamide, and blue agave extract to keep the skin hydrated and radiant but never oily.
Testing notes:
“I’m a massive fan of this hydrating gel skin tint because it’s lightweight, feels cooling upon application, and stays in place all day long. I have combination skin that sometimes gets very oily in spots, so I was hesitant at first to try this because of how many hydrators it’s packed with. The truth is, though, it has a jelly-like consistency that dries down to a matte yet glowy finish that keeps my skin looking blurred and smooth all day long, with little allowance for letting those oily spots crop up. I was also super impressed with the longevity of this pick; it lasted through a 12-hour workday with just a bit of concealer and powder,” saysGlamour’s Kilkeary.
Specs:
How to use and apply a tinted moisturizer
“The best time to apply tinted moisturizer is in the morning after washing your face and applyingface serums, if this is part of your routine,” says Dr. Stull. “Those with dryer skin may choose to apply a regular moisturizer prior to tinted moisturizer, although this may not be necessary for oilier skin types. Application of tinted moisturizer can be followed by concealer for extra coverage in particular areas, bronzer, and blush.”
Additional makeup is totally optional, though. “The beauty of tinted moisturizer is that it’s really all you might need, especially on days you don’t want to wear makeup. It’s a great all-in-one,” saysAdarsh Vijay Mudgil, MD, a board-certified dermatologist and founder of New York City’s Mudgil Dermatology, who also advises choosingtinted moisturizer with SPFif you’re not wearing sunscreen otherwise.
As for how to apply it? “With your fingertips is actually perfectly fine, though a makeup brush could help provide a more even finish,” says Dr. Mudgil. It’s simply a matter of personal preference.
What the difference between tinted moisturizer and foundation?
As for what differentiates tinted moisturizer from other types of makeup? “Tinted moisturizers offer lighter coverage in comparison withclassic foundationpreparations,” says Dr. Mudgil. “They are basically a foundation and moisturizer hybrid, which is perfect for days when full-on makeup isn’t needed or desired.”
What's the difference between tinted moisturizer and BB cream?
“Tinted moisturizers are an excellent everyday alternative to foundation, offering lightweight, natural coverage. They effectively even out skin tone, reduce redness, and blur blemishes without feeling heavy,” saysKasey Spickard, an NYC-based celebrity makeup artist.BB creams, on the other hand, may provide a bit more coverage and often have more skin care ingredients, such as SPF, hyaluronic acid and antioxidants, which can protect your skin barrier.
What's the difference between a tinted moisturizer and a skin tint?
You’ll often see the terms ‘tinted moisturizer’ and ‘skin tint’ be used interchangeably—and for the most part, they are quite similar. The main difference? Tinted moisturizer typically has more hydrating ingredients included, while a skin tint focuses on ingredients that provide coverage and protection like SPF, according to the makeup artists we spoke with.
How to apply tinted moisturizer on mature skin and aging skin
For mature skin, Spickard recommends using less product and building up slowly. “Pump one to two pumps of the product onto the back of the hand and, using your ring finger, tap into the product and then apply directly to the face (center of forehead, nose, cheeks, and chin). Then gently use your fingers to buff the product in,” he says. “And remember, less is more!”
Are there any disadvantages to tinted moisturizer?
There are no explicit disadvantages to tinted moisturizer, according to the experts we spoke with. Some may have less coverage compared to your typical foundation, but that can easily be amended by pairing your tinted moisturizer with a goodconcealer.

The most appreciated picks tend to be small upgrades or personal touches she might not buy for herself, like luxe pajamas or a journal that encourages her to take five minutes to herself.
If your partner,sister, or mom could use someself-care, a weighted sleep mask is a great pick for a Christmas or just-because gift. Looking to go the meaningful route for a best friend or aunt? Choose something that’ll remind her of her loved ones (think a digital frame preloaded with personal photos). And for anyone who’s tough to shop for, reliable standbys like high-end candles, cozy loungewear, and skin care gift sets are hard to miss.

As a wellness editor, I’ve found that upgrading to one of the best silk pillowcases is a small change that makes a noticeable difference in my sleep. Naturally breathable, temperature-regulating, and hypoallergenic, silk is frequently recommended by dermatologists and hairstylists alike for reducing frizz,breakage, and the appearance of fine lines. Unlike cotton pillowcases, which can absorb moisture, silk helps hair and skin retain hydration overnight while feeling cool to the touch and being gentle on sensitive skin. While you can incorporate silk products into your wind-down routine in many forms (includingsheetsandpajamas), a pillowcase is the simplest—and often most rewarding—place to start.
Of course, not all are created equal. Over two years, more than 20Glamourcontributors (myself included) tested dozens of options, sleeping on each for at least a month and washing them regularly. The TL;DR?Slip Pure Silk Pillowcaseremains our top pick for its high-quality silk and durability, followed by the under-$30J Jimoo Mulberry Silk Pillowcase.
Below, shop the ones we tested, including ones for curly hair, acne-prone skin, restless sleepers, and more—plus answers from dermatologists to your mostfrequently asked questions.
Amazon
Nordstrom
Free People
The Slip silk pillowcase has earned its cult-favorite status for good reason. Made from high-quality mulberry silk, it’s a consistent pick among both editors and dermatologists likeJoshua Zeichner, MD, for reducing frizz, protecting skin, and extending the life of a blowout. I own three and treasure them—the silk has the beautiful luster of a pearl and feels soft, supple, and cool without the overly slick finish some others have, almost like a butterfly wing. They keep my curly hair intact overnight, and the smooth texture, breathable weight, and elegant zip closure make it stand out in both comfort and function. While care instructions recommend hand-washing, I’ve had success using a garment bag in the washer on a cold, gentle cycle and drying on low heat. Of the silk pillowcases tested for this story, this one was least prone to long-term wrinkling. The zip closure helps pull the silk fabric taut once it’s on the pillow, giving it a cleaner, more polished look. And though they’re pricier than some others, you can use your HSA or FSA to purchase the Slip pillowcase.
Glamoursenior commerce editorJenifer Callesays:
“I love that the brand has so many gorgeous designs and the colors really pop—just looking at the glowing green on my bed makes me happy. Like most pillowcases it’s prone to drool marks, but I wash it frequently enough that it doesn’t bother me.”
GlamourcontributorDanielle Sinaysays:
“My Slip silk pillowcase has saved my skin and hair—I never wake up with red, aggravated skin anymore, and my blow-dries actually last for days,” says Sinay, who even packs it in her carry-on. “It’s so, so soft and silky, and it feels cool and lightweight on my skin. I’ve never had a problem with it getting wrinkly or losing shape in the wash.”
Fast facts
Amazon
The J Jimoo Mulberry Silk Pillowcase is a consistent bestseller inGlamourgift guides and it’s easy to see why. Despite its under-$30 price, the budget-friendly pick rivals luxury options that cost triple that amount. Made from 100% mulberry silk, it features a sleek zip closure, comes in a wide range of colors, and feels slightly less slippery than pricier versions, which many testers preferred. Its understated, logo-free design and versatile color palette make it a smart choice if you want something simple and unfussy (or are trying to find a low-key yet still luxegift for womenorgift for men). And if you’re looking to test the benefits of silk without making a big investment, this is the one to start with.
Glamourcommerce editorJake Henry Smithsays:
“This pillowcase is cool to the touch and truly soft—better than many of the expensive options I’ve tested,” says Smith. “I honestly can’t tell the difference between this and pillowcases that cost three times as much. I love the zipper closure and it’s less slippery than others so I don’t feel like I’m sliding around all night. My only gripe? It holds stains easily—but that’s silk in general.” (Just don’t wear ahair maskto bed.) “The lack of branding and the zipper closure will appeal to guys who like to keep things simple, while colors like taupe, black, white, navy, and olive fit in with masculine bedding—although it comes in many other colors too,” says Smith.
Fast facts
Quince
This colorful silk pillowcase has won over multipleGlamoureditors thanks to its combination of quality, comfort, and affordability. Made from 22-momme silk, it strikes a nice balance between softness and durability and comes in 12 vibrant shades and three sizes (standard, queen, and king). The zippered closure also keeps the case snug and helps reduce wrinkles for a neater, more polished look. I tested this one and was impressed by its quality—though it’s a tad more slippery than I prefer, it was still cooling, pretty wrinkle-resistant, and I love the price point.
GlamourcontributorAndrea Navarrosays:
“I tend to get bedhead since my hair is naturally curly, but this 22-momme silk pillowcase keeps everything in one place, tangle-free and crease-free as I sleep,” says Navarro. “At $40, it’s great value for the money (and it certainly doesn’t hurt that the brand has a 365-day return window).”
Fast facts
Amazon
Brooklinen
Brooklinen’s OEKO-TEX–certified silk case adds a polished, elevated touch to your bed, with a smooth, slightly structured finish that holds up beautifully over time. Unlike many silk options, it resists staining and maintains its sheen even after multiple washes. I appreciate its elegant envelope closure that neatly conceals the pillow edge, its subtle sheen, and how gentle and cooling it feels on my acne-prone skin. The frictionless surface helps protect my skin from seasonal dryness and prevents tugging and fine lines so I wake up looking refreshed and hydrated.
Architectural Digestcommerce editorAudrey Leesays:
“I love the luxurious feel of a silk pillowcase, and Brooklinen’s are standouts in my robust bedding collection. Their silk scrunchies are a staple in my morning and night routines as well,” says Lee. “The material has a lovely sheen, and it feels incredibly gentle on my skin and hair. Available in solids and patterns, these pillowcases still look great after several washes and run-ins with stains.”
Fast facts
Amazon
Nordstrom
Lilysilk
Lilysilk’s 19-momme pillowcase is a solid entry-level option, especially for those new to silk or on a budget. Its lighter weight gives it a soft, breathable feel that helps reduce frizz and keeps skin comfortable overnight. Two testers with curly hair found it particularly effective at preserving their styles and preventing sweat buildup during the night. While it holds up well against wrinkles initially, we found that, over time, it may start to lose some of its sheen and show minor stains. Still, it remains remarkably soft even after months of use, making it a dependable starter silk pillowcase that won’t break the bank.
Glamoursocial video editorYanise Cabrerasays:
“I just blow-dried my hair the night before and it managed to stay bouncy in the morning! Also, my neck didn’t sweat in the middle of the night—that’s a huge pro,” says Cabrera, who has curly hair. “Surprisingly, it’s not very wrinkly. I ended up laying it flat to dry, and there weren’t many wrinkles.”
Schwartz agrees: “Of all the cheaper silk pillowcases I tested, this one gets the least wrinkled and helped preserve my hair overnight—I have curly hair that I flatiron straight. However, after about six months of use, it did start to get more wrinkled after washes, and it has begun to lose its sheen and accrue some stains. It might be time to replace this pillowcase. That said, it still feels as sumptuously soft as it did on day one.”
Fast facts
Amazon
Nordstrom
Blissy’s 22-momme silk pillowcase has earned a loyal following amongGlamourstaffers and online reviewers alike. It strikes a nice balance between softness and price, and with over a dozen color options, it’s one of the most customizable picks on the market. Like Slip, it features a zippered closure that keeps the case snug and relatively wrinkle-free, but we found Blissy wrinkled more easily than most, so you might want to give it a quick steam or tuck it behind a decorative pillow if appearance matters. After extended use, we also found the silk can lose a bit of its original sheen, and one tester flagged that the zipper pull can feel sharp if left exposed.
GlamourcontributorBrie Schwartzsays:
“I’ve used my Blissy pillowcase for more than three years and it’s withstood countless washes. While it did begin to tatter and lose its luster over time, it was still supersoft and cool to the touch,” says Schwartz.
Fast facts
Amazon
If you’re looking for a step up in quality silk pillowcases without going all the way to triple-digit prices, Fishers Finery is a solid middle ground. This 25-momme mulberry silk pillowcase has a noticeable weight and softness that helps it withstand frequent washing and nightly use. While the brand also offers lower (19-momme) and higher (30-momme) options, this midrange version was our testers’ favorite for its balance of feel, durability, and price.
The slightly heavier weight gives it a luxe, hotel-like feel without being overly slick or delicate. Ultimately, it’s a great pick if you’re upgrading from a basic silk pillowcase or trying silk for the first time and want to start with something more elevated.
GlamourcontributorLindsay Schallonsays:
“After using this 25-momme pure mulberry silk pillowcase, I get why people invest in more luxurious fabrics,” says Schallon. “Not only does sleeping on this feel like the equivalent of staying the night at a fancy hotel, but waking up in the morning is significantly better because I hardly have to touch my wavy hair. The fact that my skin feels more revitalized is just the cherry on top.”
Fast facts
What are the benefits of silk pillowcases?
What’s momme count?
Momme (pronounced “mom” or “mom-may”) is a unit used to measure the weight and density of silk. The higher the momme, the more silk threads—spun from silkworm cocoons—are woven into the fabric, making it denser, shinier, and more durable. It’s similar to thread count in cotton; a higher number generally means a thicker, more substantial fabric that feels more luxurious and lasts longer.
Most experts recommend 19 momme (soft, relatively affordable) to 25 momme (top-of-the-line, more expensive). Nineteen momme is lightweight and breathable (and usually the most affordable), while 22 to 25 momme offers more durability, sheen, and density. You can go as low as 16 momme, but there are options above 20 momme in the sub-$30 range.
Is 30 momme worth it? We don’t have anything that high on this list because, frankly, it’s hard to tell the difference when the count goes above 25. If you have especially sensitive skin, prefer extra-smooth material, or want to treat yourself to some of the highest-quality silk available, feel free to buy one. (Fishers Finerymakes one that offers a great overall value.) For the highest-quality material, look for mulberry silk; the fibers are longer and more uniform, making the fabric smoother and longer-lasting.
Do silk pillowcases really help with acne?
Although one2017 studyfound that silk had a “positive influence” on back acne, which suggests that it might be helpful for breakouts elsewhere, there isn’t enough evidence to prove that silk can help with acne. At this time, more research is necessary to draw any conclusions.
What are the differences between silk and satin pillowcases?
Silk is a fiber, while satin is a weave—a particularly smooth-surfaced weave. Silk can be woven into satin fabric; artificial fibers like polyester can also be woven into satin. However, the phrasesatin pillowcaseis most often used to denote products that are woven from some material other than silk.
Polyester satin, an ultrasmooth fabric that mimics silk satin, provides many of the same friction-reducing benefits for skin and for straight and curly hair, as confirmed by our in-house testing. Man-made materials are a cost-effective and more easily washable alternative to silk pillowcases at the more expensive end of the spectrum. Silk and artificial satin can cost about the same amount, depending on the brand. If you’d rather sleep on natural fibers or want the hypoallergenic benefits of silk, stick to the real thing.
How do you clean and take care of your silk pillowcases?
“Washing or changing your pillowcase at least once a week ensures you are consistently removing bacteria, dirt, oil, hair products, skin care products, dust, and other allergens that accumulate over the week,” Dr. Salame says. “If you have acne-prone or sensitive skin, washing or changing your pillowcases every two to three days reduces the oils, bacteria, dust, and other particles that build up on the pillowcase and can be the first step to improving your skin. If you are someone who sweats overnight or uses extensive hair products, washing or changing your pillowcase every one to two days is also recommended.”
Wondering how to wash silk pillowcases? Make sure to read the product’s specific care instructions. Some can go in the washing machine on a cold, gentle cycle (ideally in a mesh delicates bag), while others require hand-washing in lukewarm or cold water. Air-drying is best, since heat can weaken the fibers and cause shrinkage, though many brands allow low-heat tumble-drying if needed. To keep them looking fresh, you can steam or lightly iron on a low setting to remove wrinkles.
As for longevity, silk pillowcases are delicate but surprisingly durable when cared for properly. Expect them to last for years, especially if you choose a higher momme count, which makes the weave denser and stronger. Most testers noticed small changes—like loss of sheen, extra wrinkling, or stains—around the six-month mark, but the pillowcases remained soft and usable well beyond that.
How did you test?
Over the course of a year and a half, over 25Glamoureditors tested dozens of silk pillowcases, sleeping on each one for at least a month and washing it regularly to see how it held up over time. We assessed everything from softness, sheen, and comfort to durability after repeated laundering, noting how each case performed night after night and whether the fit, feel, or finish changed with use.
What did you look for?
We focused on several key factors:
What other pillowcases did you test?
Since our editors have tried dozens, not all of them could make our final list, which we’re routinely updating. But if you haven’t found the exact right option yet, these may be worth a look.

trend_name: 6 Best Red-Light Therapy Masks for Glowy Skin, Vetted by Dermatologists
description:

Investing in one of the best LED face masks isn’t just about jumping on a beauty trend—it’s about bringing medical-grade skin technology into your daily routine. These devices use targeted wavelengths ofLED lightto boost collagen, improve tone and texture, and support overall skin health—all in the comfort of your own home. With so much hitting the market at once (and with many well over $300), doing your homework matters. That's why we’ve spent more than two years testing the best-sellingred-light therapy devices, plus the latest launches as soon as they drop. Despite exciting newcomers, our top pick still hasn’t changed:CurrentBody Skin LED Light Therapy Face Mask Series 2remains a standout for its high LED count and impressive full-face coverage that delivers faster, more consistent results. Of course, it’s not one-mask-fits-all. The right choice depends on your skin concerns, your routine, and your budget. Ahead, we break down our top seven picks, includingthe few we didn’t love enoughto recommend.
CurrentBody
Amazon
We’ve extensivelyreviewed CurrentBody LED Face Mask, which was a winner inGlamour’s 2023 Beauty & Wellness Awards, is Kim Kardashian’s favorite, and earned a strong recommendation byY. Claire Chang, MD, a board-certified cosmetic dermatologist at Union Square Laser Dermatology in New York City. The brand’s Series 2 is an upgraded mask with 236 LED bulbs (compared with the original 132) in red, near-infrared, and new-to-the-mix deep near-infrared wavelengths. “Infrared light, at 830 nanometers, causes collagen stimulation, plus reduction in signs of photoaging, which include wrinkles, laxity, and hyperpigmentation,” saysDan Belkin, MD, a board-certified dermatologist in New York City.Glamoursenior beauty editorAriana Yaptangco, who has tested multiple red-light therapy face masks in her career says she was impressed with the long-term results. “After just a month of using this mask, I noticed my skin looked smoother and more radiant.” She also found it to be the most comfortable she’s tried. “It was simple to adjust to get the right fit, and the mask stayed put while I was walking around,” she says.
GlamourUK-based senior commerce editorSophie Cockettsays:
“The design of the CurrentBody Series 2 mask stands out among the rest. It's made from cutting-edge poured liquid silicone, which issomuch more comfortable than its predecessor. When I reviewed Series 1, I noted that I had to use a claw clip to keep the mask in place—and that it hurt a little bit after 10 minutes of use. Now the multiway straps can be adjusted to all head shapes and ensure the mask stays put comfortably.”—Sophie Cockett,GlamourUK-based senior commerce editor
Glamourcommerce writerAlanna Martine Kilkearysays:
“This red-light therapy mask is one that has given me results faster than any other red light product I’ve tried. It fits snugly, doesn’t slip or slide, and doesn’t feel heavy even if you’re doing everyday tasks like washing the dishes or cleaning your apartment. I like that after I pull it off, my skin seem to almost immediately depuff; you can see a clear difference in my before and after. I like to use this every other week, and especially before a big event.”—Alanna Martine Kilkeary,Glamourcommerce writer
Fast facts
Amazon
Shark is known to be one of the more innovative brands in the beauty space (just look at its viralSmoothStyleorFacial Pro Growtools), and now it has entered the LED mask space with this brand-new launch. Despite its structured design, the brand’s mask is made to be more comfortable than most, thanks to features like forehead padding, silicone eye shields, and adjustable straps. What really sets this device apart, though, is its built-in undereye treatment (a first in this space!), which delivers a cryotherapy-like cooling sensation. You can use the remote to track your progress on three main concerns: Blemish Repair Mode (8 minutes), Better Ageing Mode (6 minutes), or Skin Sustain Mode (4 minutes). You can use one or combine them depending on your skin concern. Just keep in mind that the battery doesn’t last long. You’ll probably have to charge it once a week. The combination of the features, design, remote, and comfortable wear make it a value for what you’re getting compared to other LED face masks that might offer only red light.
Glamoursenior commerce editorJenifer Callesays:
“It helped revive my puffy eyes in just five minutes one morning after not getting enough sleep. For the LED treatment, I used red, blue, and purple light. I appreciated having the blue LED option during my monthly hormonal breakouts—I feel like I don’t break out as much as I normally do thanks to consistent blue light use. I also love that it tracks all my sessions, so I can monitor and note any improvements—and I have! My skin texture is noticeably smoother and more radiant whenever I use it. I like to use the Blemish Repair Mode because it offers blue, red, and infrared (pink) to target texture, redness, or acne. Since I’m constantly testing products, using this LED face mask feels like a reset.” Jenifer Calle,Glamoursenior commerce editor
Fast facts
Nordstrom
Omnilux
“This mask has been shown to reduce visible signs of aging and redness,” says Dr. Chang. The silicone mask is flexible enough to comfortably mold to many different face shapes. Our testers found the red-light therapy mask easy to operate via its remote that hangs from one side. Simply toggle it on and let it do its 10 minutes of treatment. Though it only operates on two wavelengths, if you want a competitively priced LED device that’s comfortable enough to wear while relaxing or multitasking, this is it. “After three months of consistent use, I noticed my dry skin looked more alive, and I had fewer fine lines in between Botox appointments,” saysGlamourcontributor Brigitte Earley, who regularly used this mask when it first came out back in late 2020. It did wonders for testers as a men’s LED face mask too (though the brand carries a separate siliconeOmnilux Mendevice, designed for slightly thicker skin).
Glamoursenior commerce editorJenifer Callesays:
“I used Omnilux consistently for just a few weeks, and it brought life back to my dull skin. The combination of indoor heat and cold winter air can be harsh on my skin. There’s been tightness and rough texture, which hasn’t helped the lines on my forehead. After using this face mask, I noticed that they were less visible and my skin has a boost of radiance,” saysGlamour’s Calle.
Glamourglobal editorial directorSamantha Barrysays:
“My facialist, Kristyn Smith, introduced me to the Omnilux two years ago right before theMet Gala, and I haven’t looked back since. I use it every day religiously, even putting it on my scalp and chest on the weekends, when I have more time to spare. It’s so easy to use and charge; I just cleanse my face and enjoy my 10 minutes of red-light therapy at home—or a little self-care time, as I see it.”—Samantha Barry,Glamourglobal editorial director
Fast facts
Amazon
Nordstrom
Sephora
This at-home device puts a big emphasis on blue light and targeting acne while working to address antiaging concerns at the same time. “It works better for inflammatory blemishes, but it can help heal and restore all acne-prone skin,” saysJessica Weiser, MD, a board-certified dermatologist at Weiser Skin MD, who uses this therapy in her NYC office. We also like that the mask is entirely wireless so there’s nothing hanging down from your face. You simply press the button on the top of the mask to toggle through the three different light settings: red, blue, or a combination.
GlamourcontributorSarah Wureviewed theDr. Dennis Gross LED Face Maskand reported, “It’s a derm visit, spa day, and photo op in one.” Another tester noted significant improvements in her skin while using the mask three times per week. “There was a clear reduction in my acne, and after two months I noticed I was able to scrunch the fine lines on my forehead less—similar to the effect of Botox,” she said.
Glamourcommerce editorMalia Griggssays:
“I’ve owned this face mask for almost a year and truly think it’s a savior for my skin. I put it on before bed at least three times per week and usually put on a TV show in the background while it cycles through the blue and red modes (my two most frequently used wavelengths). I like that the lights aren’t so bright that they hurt my eyes and that the treatment times are so fast that I sometimes do them a couple times. The blue mode is my favorite, especially as I deal with frequent breakouts. It seems to calm my inflammation and to adequately kill the bacteria so that my zits don’t return. I’ve also noticed a tightening of my skin, especially in my undereye area. My only complaint is that the mask is quite heavy, and its strap is very short, so it tends to unravel and pop off my face if I’m not careful.”—Malia Griggs,Glamourcommerce editor
Fast facts
Amazon
Sephora
HigherDOSE
The standout feature of theHigherDose face maskis its design—it’s extremely flexible, so it expertly contours to the curves of the face. It also has three straps—two on the side and one on top—so you can get a snug fit no matter your face shape. It comes with extra plugs for international getaways, making it a travel-friendly option, and is cheaper than most of the other face masks that we tried.
Glamour’s Calle says:
“It’s lightweight and bendable, and has three straps (many other brands have only two) for a more secure fit. The comfortability of a face mask is very important, because if you don’t like wearing it, you might skip it, and it’ll end up collecting dust in your beauty cabinet. After only a few weeks of using the HigherDose mask, I noticed that my skin felt a lot smoother and softer, but the biggest result was that the hormonal acne around my chin and jawline during my period significantly improved.”—J.C.
Fast facts
Amazon
Nordstrom
Therabody just launched the new TheraFace Mask Glo last October, and I was among the first to give it a whirl. It immediately struck me as a lighter, more wearable version of theoriginal TheraFace LED mask. The fit is excellent, thanks to an adjustable strap that goes over the top of your head and around the sides, so it really stays put. But one of my favorite features is that the inside of the mask is contoured with a raised panel around the eyes that allows the LEDs to sit closer to the skin. That detail makes a big difference for targeting the fine lines, wrinkles, and early signs of sagging in the most delicate area of the face.
GlamourcontributorBrigitt Earleysays:
“I love that the mask cycles through multiple light modes in a single 12-minute treatment, so you don’t have to choose between benefits—you get red, blue, and infrared in one go. That said, I do wish the vibrations were more soothing. I tested it during a pretty significant headache and didn’t get much tension relief. And because there’s no nose or mouth opening, the airflow feels a little limited, which made the experience slightly stuffier than I’d prefer.”—Brigitt Earley,Glamourcontributor
Fast facts
LED face mask comparison chart
How do LED face masks work exactly?
LED (light-emitting diode) masks use specific wavelengths of light that penetrate deep into the skin to stimulate natural repair processes, saysJoshua Zeichner, MD, a board-certified dermatologist and associate professor of dermatology at Mount Sinai Hospital in New York City. This can help improve skin texture, tone, and elasticity, while addressing concerns like acne (and acne scars),dark spots,rosacea, eczema,hyperpigmentation, redness, fine lines, wrinkles, and even psoriasis.
You might be wondering, “Do they actually work?” Well, according to numerous studies “LED light therapy can aid in skin healing, improve chronic conditions like acne and rosacea, and enhance overall skin health,” saysViktoryia Kazlosukaya, MD, PhD, a board-certified dermatologist in New York City. “That said, the results are often modest and gradual. It’s essential to set realistic expectations—LED masks arenota substitute for professional skin care, cosmetic procedures performed by board-certified dermatologists, or prescription medications. They are best viewed as a supplementary tool in a broader skin care routine.”
How to choose an LED face mask
When choosing an LED face mask, start by considering the light modes, design, and safety features.
Light modes:Most masks use red light or a combination of red and infrared, but some include blue or amber lights to target specific concerns. Common wavelengths (measured in nanometers) and benefits include:
Design:Masks can be structured and molded or flexible silicone. Structured masks may sit closer to the skin, while flexible masks are often lighter and more comfortable. Fully wireless designs are ideal, though some masks include clips to help offset bulky battery packs or controllers.
FDA clearance:Safety and efficacy are critical. All the masks on our list are FDA-cleared, a point emphasized by both theMayo Clinicand the dermatologists we consulted.
Tips to start using an LED face mask
When using an LED face mask, start by cleansing your skin thoroughly to remove makeup, dirt, and oil, and make sure it’s completely dry—any products left on the skin can interfere with light absorption. For best results, fit the mask so it sits flush against your face, then turn it on.
Usage frequency and session length depend on the product’s instructions. Most masks recommend 10-minute sessions, though some can be as short as 3 minutes or as long as 20 minutes. After your session, you can immediately follow with your regular skin care products, such as serums and moisturizers. There’s no required time of day—morning or evening works equally well.
Are LED face masks safe for your eyes?
Yes, red-light therapy face masks are generally safe for your eyes. “Most lights aren’t likely to damage the eyes, unless we are talking about ultraviolet light (UV) or staring at intense bright light (think of looking directly at the sun during a solar eclipse),” saysMichael Brusco, MD, FADCS, medical director and surgeon at Brusco Vision in Washington, DC.
If the mask feels very bright or causes glare, it’s usually more annoying than harmful. If you have sensitive eyes, make sure to select a mask with built-in eye protection. And if you experience any dryness, Dr. Brusco recommends using eye drops.
Can you overdo it with an LED face mask?
You can overuse an LED face mask, though any side effects are unlikely to be significant. “Overusing an LED face mask can lead to skin irritation, dryness, or sensitivity,” saysKristina Collins, MD, a board-certified dermatologist in Austin, Texas. “While LED light therapy is generally safe for most skin types, excessive use—beyond the recommended frequency—does not necessarily yield better results.” Follow your product’s instructions, and if you experience burning, stinging, or redness, stop using the mask and consult a dermatologist.
How long does it take for LED face masks to work?
LED face masks generally begin to show noticeable results within three to four weeks of consistent use (consistencyis the key word here). Initial improvements often include smoother skin texture and reduced acne inflammation. For more significant benefits, such as diminished fine lines and enhanced skin tone, it may take two to three months of regular treatment. Results will vary based on your skin type, the concerns you’re trying to address, and the quality of the device.
The most effective LED face masks emit wavelengths of red light between 620 and 750 nanometers, blue light between 405 and 420 nm, and infrared light between 760 and 1,400 nm, saysAnna Chacon, MD, a board-certified dermatologist in Miami. Consistent use (approximately three to five times per week) at this level should produce results within about two months, she says.
Who shouldnotuse LED light therapy?
Avoid using LED light therapy if you have any active inflammatory skin conditions or are immediately post-procedure from things like microneedling or laser resurfacing, saysMeredith Price, MD, a board-certified dermatologist in Grosse Pointe, Michigan. “I always advise caution to patients with melasma, as this is a light- and heat-sensitive condition. I’ve seenred-light therapy maskshelp melasma, and I have also seen them worsen it,” she says. “Most LED devices have not been assessed for safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding, so I advise avoidance during that time as well.”
Is LED light therapy safe for dark skin?
“LED masks are generally safe for dark skin,” saysAsmi Berry, DO, FAAD, a board-certified dermatologist in Los Angeles. “Unlike lasers, LED light doesn’t target melanin, so there’s no increased risk of burns or hyperpigmentation for deeper skin tones.” She advises sticking with FDA-cleared, dermatologist-recommended brands, which tend to be more extensively tested on all skin types. “If you’re using an at-home mask, make sure it doesn’t get hot. For sensitive skin, too much heat or irritation could trigger inflammation, which in darker skin can sometimes lead to post-inflammatory pigment changes,” she says.
How we tested
We test red-light therapy face masks by surveying popular, well-reviewed devices and consulting dermatologists for their personal and professional recommendations. Our testing includes a wide range of FDA-cleared masks, and we do not prioritize brands we have affiliations with over others. Testers—men and women over 30 with different skin concerns—evaluate each mask for comfort, design, treatment time, and ease of use.
Because red-light therapy takes time to show results, we encourage testers to keep and use the masks consistently over several months (and ideally longer), giving us a clear picture of long-term performance and effectiveness. We also continuously test new and next-generation models to update this guide with fresh insights on efficacy, durability, and overall experience.
